memoryArtifacts.headline: A Brief History
memoryArtifacts.description: WILLIAM de CLAYTON was born about 1060 in Normandy. William took over as Lord of Clayton after his older brother was slain when he was in the war against Malcolm, King of Scotland.

popularStory.headline: A Brief History

zh.lifeSummary: 當Baron William de Clayton I於1060年出生在英國藍卡爾克雷頓時，他的父親Lord Robert de Clayton25歲，母親Margaret Osdalden26歲。他於1079年與Mary Hyde在英國英格蘭藍卡爾克雷頓 勒 波斯結婚。他們至少是5個兒子的父母。他去世於1141年2月2日在英國波藍德利特享年81歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Clayton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places, in Yorkshire, Lancashire, Staffordshire, and Sussex, named Clayton, from Old English clǣg ‘clay’ + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’.

variants.name: Cleeton
meanings: English (Shropshire and Worcestershire): variant of   Clayton  .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Battle of Hastings
historicEvents.description: Battle of Hastings was fought between the Norman-French army and the battle-wary English army on October 14, 1066. Duke William of Normandy believed he was the rightful king rather than Harold, who had been crowned king several months earlier. Harold was killed in battle and William was crowned king on Christmas Day 1066.

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When Baron William de Clayton I was born in 1060, in Clayton, Lancashire, England, his father, Lord Robert de Clayton, was 25 and his mother, Margaret Osdalden, was 26. He married Mary Hyde in 1079, in Clayton le Moors, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons. He died on 2 February 1141, in Penrith, Cumberland, England, at the age of 81.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Baron William de Clayton I est né en 1060, à Clayton, Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, Lord Robert de Clayton, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Margaret Osdalden, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Mary Hyde en 1079, à Clayton le Moors, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils. Il est décédé le 2 février 1141, à Penrith, Cumberland, Angleterre, à l’âge de 81 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Baron William de Clayton Iが1060年にイギリスランカシュアイアクレイトンで生まれた時，父Lord Robert de Claytonは25歳，母Margaret Osdaldenは26歳であった。1079年にイギリスイギリスランカシュアイアクレイトン ル ムールズでMary Hydeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子の親であった。彼は1141年2月2日にイギリスクウンバーランドペンリスで81歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Baron William de Clayton I(이)가 1060년 영국 랭카실르 클레이턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Lord Robert de Clayton(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Osdalden(은)는 26세였다. 그는 1079년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 클레이턴 일 무르스에서 Mary Hyde(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1141년 2월 2일 영국 c벌랜드 펜리스에서 81세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Baron William de Clayton I родился в 1060 г. в местности клеитон, ланкашир, Англия, его отцу по имени Lord Robert de Clayton было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Osdalden, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Mary Hyde в 1079 г. в местности клеитон л мурс, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей. Он умер 2 февраля 1141 г. в местности пенрит, кумбирланд, Англия в возрасте 81 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Baron William de Clayton I nació en 1060, en Clayton, Lancashire, Inglaterra, su papá, Lord Robert de Clayton, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Margaret Osdalden, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Mary Hyde en 1079, en Clayton le Moors, Lancashire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos. Murió el 2 de febrero de 1141, en Penrith, Cumberland, Inglaterra, a la edad de 81 años.
